name: La Defensa del Sur and Vinalopó Towns Loop
name_original: Novelda-Petrer – Circular por la Vía Augusta y el Camino del Cid
description: This difficult 28.2-mile touring cycling loop, La Defensa del Sur and Vinalopó Towns Loop, offers historical sites and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Novelda offers diverse landscapes, from wide fields and Mediterranean forests to barren hills. The region is characterized by the Vinalopó River, which features prominently in several routes, and the Vía Verde del Maigmó, a converted railway line with impressive tunnels and viaducts. Cyclists can experience varied terrain, including olive groves and mountain views, making it a compelling destination for outdoor enthusiasts. Best touring cycling routes around Novelda The most popular touring cycling route is La Defensa del Sur and Vinalopó Towns Loop, a 28.2 miles (45.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects several Vinalopó towns, offering a blend of cultural and natural scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is River crossing – Vinalopó River loop from Novelda, a difficult 20.8 miles (33.6 km) path. This route follows the Vinalopó River, providing scenic views along its course. Local touring cyclists also love the El Maigmó Greenway – First tunnel from Agost loop from Novelda, a 36.6 miles (58.8 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde del Maigmó, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. This route features impressive tunnels and viaducts. Touring cycling around Novelda is defined by varied terrain, the Vinalopó River, and dedicated greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Novelda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Novelda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Novelda?
A: Novelda offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 45 options to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Novelda?
A: The region around Novelda features diverse landscapes, including wide fields, Mediterranean forests, and barren hills. You'll often find routes traversing picturesque olive groves and offering magnificent views of the surrounding mountains. The Vinalopó River also plays a prominent role, with many routes following its course.

Q: Are there any greenways or dedicated cycle paths in Novelda?
A: Yes, a significant draw for touring cyclists is the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Maigmó&lt;/strong&gt;. This former railway line has been converted into an adventurous cycle path, featuring impressive tunnels and viaducts. One popular route that utilizes this greenway is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10332232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Maigmó Greenway – First tunnel from Agost loop from Novelda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Novelda?
A: While many routes in Novelda are rated as difficult, there are still options for less experienced cyclists. The region offers 5 easy and 15 moderate touring cycling routes, providing gentler terrain and shorter distances suitable for beginners or family outings. These routes often follow flatter sections along the Vinalopó River or through agricultural areas.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in Novelda?
A: Beyond the Vinalopó River and olive groves, you can discover various natural highlights. For instance, you might encounter interesting geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/658102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pocico Alonso&lt;/a&gt; or explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3369584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Caballo&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features several peaks, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3072247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Silla del Cid Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there historical or cultural landmarks accessible by touring bike in Novelda?
A: Absolutely. Novelda is rich in history. You can cycle to the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Santa Maria Magdalena&lt;/a&gt;, an architectural marvel perched on a hill. Routes also incorporate historical paths like the Vía Augusta and the Camino del Cid, adding a cultural dimension to your ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31474288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Five-Arch Bridge – Vinalopó River Crossing loop from Novelda-Aspe&lt;/a&gt; is another notable man-made structure you can encounter.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Novelda?
A: The touring cycling routes in Novelda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the Vinalopó River, and the unique experience of riding along dedicated greenways like the Vía Verde del Maigmó.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes around Novelda?
A: Yes, Novelda offers several longer routes for touring cyclists. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10332232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Maigmó Greenway – First tunnel from Agost loop from Novelda&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 59 km, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33785795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago – Old Town of Monforte del Cid loop from Novelda-Aspe&lt;/a&gt; is over 46 km, providing substantial distances for extended rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Novelda?
A: Novelda generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are typically mild, making it a viable destination year-round, though checking local weather forecasts is always advisable.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Novelda are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952452924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Defensa del Sur and Vinalopó Towns Loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31714338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;River crossing – Vinalopó River loop from Novelda&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient circular journeys.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in Novelda?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Novelda has plenty to offer, with 28 routes rated as difficult. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and longer distances, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.smarttour/e952452924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Defensa del Sur and Vinalopó Towns Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 450 meters of ascent over 45 km.
tips: The church "Santuario de Santa María Magdalena" is considered a very unique and outstanding work within the Valencian modernism. It is no coincidence that it bears a certain resemblance to the Sagrada Familia Antonio Gaudís in Barcelona. José Sala Sala, the architect of the pilgrimage church, was clearly inspired by the world-famous building by Antonio Gaudí.

The castle is located on the highest point of Petrer. It was built by the Moors at the end of the 12th century. It offers a wide, beautiful panoramic view over the flatter "level". A steep path leads up to the castle, which can be used by bike. Maybe you have to push a bit uphill. A detour through the small streets of the old town, below the castle, is worth it!

faqs.question: How challenging is the La Defensa del Sur and Vinalopó Towns Loop?
faqs.answer: This route is generally classified as low to medium difficulty for cycling, but it does include sections that demand good fitness. While much of it is accessible for touring and gravel bikes, some mountainous segments can be quite strenuous, such as the ascent to Bocairent, and might require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse landscape. You'll encounter highly urbanized sections, agricultural tracks, and paved "vías verdes" (repurposed railway lines). There are also more mountainous areas with trails. Expect rural tracks with firm surfaces, comfortable lanes, and tarmacked roads. The trail along the Vinalopó River often features an arid landscape alongside cultivated margins and sand formations.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop is rich in historical and natural sights. You can visit the impressive Sanctuary of Saint Mary Magdalene and the historic Castillo de Petrer. Other highlights include La Albufera Natural Park, extensive Valencian orchards, numerous medieval castles (part of an Almohad defensive line), charming historic towns like Bocairent (described as a "city carved out of stone"), and the extensive palm groves around Elche, including the UNESCO World Heritage Site Palmeral of Elche.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The recommended time to undertake this journey is during autumn, winter, or spring. This avoids the intense summer heat, which can make cycling uncomfortable in this region.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other well-known paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the La Defensa del Sur (45%), Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Alicante (34%), Camino de Santiago del Sureste (34%), Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana (30%), and the Camino del Cid (Senderista) (28%).

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many outdoor trails in Spain, specific rules for this particular cycling route regarding dogs are not explicitly stated. It's advisable to check local regulations for each town or natural park you pass through, especially concerning leash requirements and access to historical sites.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the La Defensa del Sur and Vinalopó Towns Loop itself. However, some historical sites, castles, or natural parks along the route might have their own entrance fees if you choose to visit them.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vinalopó Towns Loop?
faqs.answer: As this is a loop route passing through multiple towns, you have several options for parking. You can typically find parking in the towns along the route, such as Novelda or Petrer, which serve as convenient starting points. Look for public parking areas within these towns.

faqs.question: What type of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, which includes agricultural tracks, paved vías verdes, and some mountainous trails, a touring bicycle or a gravel bike is highly recommended. These bikes offer the versatility needed to comfortably handle both paved and unpaved sections of the route.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay overnight along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through numerous charming towns and cities, you will find ample opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Towns like Novelda, Petrer, Bocairent, Villena, Elche, and Orihuela offer various options for refreshments and overnight stays, allowing you to break up the journey.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The "La Defensa del Sur and Vinalopó Towns Loop" is designed as a circular route, meaning it is a loop trail. This allows you to start and finish in the same location without retracing your path.
